title: Support label filters for podman pod ps
body:
Update the podman pod ps command to support filtering by labels.
This brings the command in line with the documentation as well as
the functionality by the containers equivalent podman ps.

Signed-off-by: Stefano Pogliani <stefano@spogliani.net>

Information from the issue template:

/kind bug

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Execute `podman pod ps --filter 'label=some.label'`

**Describe the results you received:**
The command fails:

```
$ podman pod ps --filter 'label=some.label'
Error: invalid filter: label is an invalid filter
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
A, possibly empty, list of pods with the label set (for the `label=KEY` version) or with the label matching `VALUE` (for the `label=KEY=VALUE` version).

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.8.1-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional notes:**
I've tried my best to follow the [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) guidelines but tests seem to fail on master itself.
Even for the subset of tests I worked near, some tests seem to fail on master even without my changes.

Please also note that I'm very inexperienced with golang.

comments.body:
Not sure why the tests are failing as they look unrelated to my changes.
I'm happy to try and fix them by I may need some guidance.

A rebase should fix special_test_bindings.

Fedora/Ubuntu may be legitimate bugs.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *stefano-pogliani* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I've tried re-basing on master a few times and there are less test failures there are still some which I don't really understand.

I suspect the main one is [this](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5591808457572352/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-exec-preserve-fds-sanity-check--2):
```

Error: error saving container b00f26b393828e5b1ebcbd092eb4701a48f757ab836e0ec3ac933ad246a62925 state: bad file descriptor
```

The other two seems to be timeouts, maybe caused by podman DB corruption?

Hello @rhatdan,

It looks like the bot has not applied the lgtm label (although it did apply the approve one).
The `tide` check suggests it is preventing the PR from merging.

Cheers

Thanks for that.

Many thanks also for the awesome tool that podman is :smile: :fireworks:
(especially the rootless containers part)
